column2: Table + grain decisions the dictionaries do not settle mechanically. Each row states its
evidence. Nothing here is inferred from a variable NAME.
(a) ROSMAP — the codebook says "All longitudinal data sets are organized by projid + visit or
fu_year", but its Clinical/Pathology section headers do not survive PDF text extraction
(`spanish`, a demographic, lands under 'Pathology' purely by text position). So each ROSMAP
column's grain is set here from its own description.
(b) Three elements no AMP table claimed. They are AMP-AD and their source_file names a
DiverseCohorts file, so they are assigned to that subject-grained table.
table: the file the column lives in

column5: Age of participant at time of clinical visit — one value per visit; ROSMAP: "longitudinal data sets are organized by projid + visit".
ROSMAP codebook: "A clinical diagnosis of cognitive status is rendered at every assessment." Explicitly per-visit.
Germline genotype. Fixed for life; cannot vary by visit.
Years of Education — ROSMAP records it once at baseline (Continuous, no visit qualifier).
Spanish/Hispanic/Latino origin — self-reported ethnicity, recorded once at baseline. (NOT pathology; the PDF section header is a text-extraction artifact.)
Age at Death — a person has exactly one. Cannot appear on a visit row: at the time of a clinical visit the participant is alive.
Age at first cycle where an Alzheimer's dementia diagnosis was rendered — a derived, once-per-person summary over the visit series.
ROSMAP: "Clinical consensus diagnosis of cognitive status AT TIME OF DEATH ... all available clinical data were reviewed." One per person, determined post-mortem. Contrast dcfdx, which is per-visit.
Braak stage — measured on brain tissue at autopsy. One per person.
CERAD score — neuritic plaque density measured at autopsy. One per person.
Post-Mortem Interval — hours between death and autopsy. One per person, by definition.
source_file names DiverseCohorts. A derived autopsy outcome (Braak + CERAD thresholds) — one per person.
source_file names DiverseCohorts. "Mayo Clinic neuropathological diagnosis" — determined at autopsy, one per person.

No source_file in the dictionary. "Brodmann area designation for brain tissue sample" — a property of the post-mortem tissue sample, so it belongs with the neuropathology table. FLAGGED: this is the one assignment the dictionary does not state; confirm or move it.
